fundamentals.name: Capital One Financial Corporation
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Capital One Financial Corporation operates as the financial services holding company for the Capital One, National Association, which engages in the provision of various financial products and services in the United States, Canada, and the United Kingdom. It operates through three segments: Credit Card, Consumer Banking, and Commercial Banking. The company accepts checking accounts, money market deposits, negotiable order of withdrawals, savings deposits, time deposits, and sweep accounts. Its loan products include credit card and personal loans; auto and retail banking loans; and commercial and multifamily real estate, and commercial and industrial loans. The company offers credit and debit card products; bank lending; and provides advisory, capital markets, net interchange, treasury management, and depository services. It serves consumers, small businesses, and commercial clients through digital channels, branches, cafés, and other distribution channels located in New York, Louisiana, Texas, Maryland, Virginia, New Jersey, and the District of Columbia. Capital One Financial Corporation was founded in 1988 and is headquartered in McLean, Virginia.

judgment.summary: A company with strong revenue and earnings growth in the last quarter, but facing a high P/E, declining full-year earnings estimates, and low profitability metrics.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed consensus by 3.3%; next earnings date is 2026-07-21.
positives: Quarterly earnings growth 54.8% shows recent operational strength.
Revenue growth 46.3% YoY indicates significant top-line expansion.
Forward P/E of 7.76 suggests the stock is priced for growth relative to its earnings.
Analyst consensus is 'buy' with a mean target of $255.19, implying upside potential.
Total cash of $79.39B provides a substantial liquidity buffer.
negatives: Trailing P/E of 57.68 is very high, suggesting current earnings are not supporting the valuation.
Earnings growth YoY is negative at -3.2%, and analyst estimates for the current fiscal year are only 0.4%.
Profit margin of 8.9% and Return on Equity of 3.3% are relatively low.
Dividend payout ratio of 86.1% is high, potentially limiting future dividend growth or reinvestment.
Last quarter's EPS missed estimates by 3.3%.

news.paragraph: Capital One Financial Corporation (COF) saw investors reject a golden parachute proposal amid ongoing risk focus, while a recent article questioned the company's new capital allocation playbook regarding dividends and the Discover integration. Separately, TD Cowen offered its perspective on the company, and Goldman Sachs noted Broadcom (AVGO) as a strong AI infrastructure bet, though this is not directly related to COF. The next earnings date is July 21st.
